speaker: DUKE
text: So that from point to point now have you heard

speaker: DUKE
text: The fundamental reasons of this war,

speaker: DUKE
text: Whose great decision hath much blood let forth

speaker: DUKE
text: And more thirsts after.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: Holy seems the quarrel

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: Upon your Grace’s part, black and fearful

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: On the opposer.

speaker: DUKE
text: Therefore we marvel much our cousin France

speaker: DUKE
text: Would in so just a business shut his bosom

speaker: DUKE
text: Against our borrowing prayers.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              LORD
text: Good my lord,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              LORD
text: The reasons of our state I cannot yield

speaker:
SECOND
               
              LORD
text: But like a common and an outward man

speaker:
SECOND
               
              LORD
text: That the great figure of a council frames

speaker:
SECOND
               
              LORD
text: By self-unable motion; therefore dare not

speaker:
SECOND
               
              LORD
text: Say what I think of it, since I have found

speaker:
SECOND
               
              LORD
text: Myself in my incertain grounds to fail

speaker:
SECOND
               
              LORD
text: As often as I guessed.

speaker: DUKE
text: Be it his pleasure.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: But I am sure the younger of our nation,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: That surfeit on their ease, will day by day

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: Come here for physic.

speaker: DUKE
text: Welcome shall they be,

speaker: DUKE
text: And all the honors that can fly from us

speaker: DUKE
text: Shall on them settle. You know your places well.

speaker: DUKE
text: When better fall, for your avails they fell.

speaker: DUKE
text: Tomorrow to th’ field.
